title: Costs of the First Flat
scenario: You have found a flat for ₹15,000/month rent. The landlord wants a two-month security deposit (₹30,000) and the broker is asking a one-month fee (₹15,000). A friend offers to introduce you to a no-broker listing nearby, but it means waiting another two weeks and a few more viewings.

choices.label: Wait two weeks and take the no-broker flat to skip the ₹15,000 fee
choices.lesson: Broker fees are negotiable or avoidable. A short wait can save a full month's rent that adds nothing to your living standard.

choices.label: Grab this flat now and pay deposit plus broker fee
choices.lesson: Convenience has a price. The security deposit is refundable later, but the broker fee is gone for good, so weigh urgency against the cost.
